All work under this OMO must be done using safe work practices, occupant protection, training, and certification pursuant to EPA’s Renovation,
Repair and Painting (RRP) rule under 40 CFR Part 745 Subpart E and lead-based paint abatement activities under 40 CFR Part 745 Subpart L (where
abatement/permanent removal is performed), and New York City Admin Code § 27-2056.11. The work scope requires that the firm be EPA-certified to
perform both RRP activities and abatement activities. Based on the work scope, workers assigned must be individually EPA-certified Renovators
(where work is renovation) or Lead Abatement Workers/Supervisors (where work is abatement/permanent removal). If the work scope included
abatement/permanent removal, the contractor must file appropriately with EPA. In addition, the contractor must comply with the tenant notification and
record-keeping requirements pursuant to RRP and perform a cleaning verification. HPD may audit the cleaning verification and tenant notification
records. HPD will conduct a dust wipe clearance test at the conclusion of the work to confirm proper cleaning has been completed.

All work under this OMO must be done using safe work practices, occupant protection, training, and certification pursuant to EPA’s Renovation,
Repair and Painting (RRP) rule under 40 CFR Part 745 Subpart E and lead-based paint abatement activities under 40 CFR Part 745 Subpart L (where
abatement/permanent removal is performed), and New York City Admin Code § 27-2056.11. The work scope requires that the firm be EPA-certified to
perform both RRP activities and abatement activities. Based on the work scope, workers assigned must be individually EPA-certified Renovators
(where work is renovation) or Lead Abatement Workers/Supervisors (where work is abatement/permanent removal). If the work scope included
abatement/permanent removal, the contractor must file appropriately with EPA. In addition, the contractor must comply with the tenant notification and
record-keeping requirements pursuant to RRP and perform a cleaning verification. HPD may audit the cleaning verification and tenant notification
records. HPD will conduct a dust wipe clearance test at the conclusion of the work to confirm proper cleaning has been completed.
